definition: When SPECTRA needs a thresholded graph rather than top-k neighbors, use similarity-join methods such as prefix filtering, bitmap filters, locality-sensitive filtering, or distributed joins to enumerate all train-eval pairs above a threshold, then verify each candidate with the exact similarity.

scale_profile.best_for: Building binary or thresholded weighted SPECTRA graphs where every edge above a threshold should be recovered.
scale_profile.typical_upper_bound: Millions to billions of candidate records in optimized implementations or distributed systems.
scale_profile.avoid_when: Only nearest-neighbor overlap is needed; top-k ANN is usually simpler.

tunable_parameters.name: threshold
tunable_parameters.purpose: Defines the emitted SPECTRA graph edge set.

tunable_parameters.name: prefix_ordering
tunable_parameters.purpose: Controls filtering power through rare-token ordering.

tunable_parameters.name: approximate_recall_target
tunable_parameters.purpose: Optional recall target for randomized joins.

quality_gates: Verify candidate pairs with the exact similarity function before output.
Report threshold, candidate reduction, and exact or approximate recall guarantee.
Check whether threshold choice leaves enough edges for SPECTRA split construction.
failure_modes: Low thresholds can create too many candidate pairs.
Approximate joins require recall calibration before interpreting missing edges.
Prefix filtering performs poorly when rare tokens are absent or uninformative.
example_usage: python set_similarity_threshold_join.py --train train.csv --eval eval.csv --out pairwise_similarity.csv --set-col terms --threshold 0.5

citations.title: Scalable and Robust Set Similarity Join
citations.evidence: Contrasts exact prefix-filtering joins with randomized set similarity joins and reports recall/speed tradeoffs.

citations.title: Bitmap Filter: Speeding up Exact Set Similarity Joins with Bitwise Operations
citations.evidence: Uses bitwise filters to accelerate exact set similarity joins.
